concepts.lesson: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time and a reference point. If the position of an object changes over time, the object is said to be in motion.
concepts.real_example: A moving car on a road is an example of motion.
key_points: Objects may appear moving or stationary depending on observer

concepts.lesson: Distance is the total path length travelled by an object during its motion. It does not depend on direction and is always a positive quantity.
concepts.real_example: If a person walks around a park and covers 500 m, the total path travelled is the distance.

concepts.lesson: Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final positions of an object. It has both magnitude and direction.
concepts.real_example: If a student walks 20 m east and returns to the starting point, displacement is zero.
key_points: May be zero even when distance is not zero

concepts.lesson: Uniform motion occurs when an object travels equal distances in equal intervals of time.
concepts.real_example: A train moving at a constant speed on a straight track shows uniform motion.
key_points: Equal distances in equal intervals of time
Distance-time graph is a straight line

concepts.lesson: Non-uniform motion occurs when an object travels unequal distances in equal intervals of time.
concepts.real_example: A car moving in city traffic usually shows non-uniform motion.
key_points: Unequal distances in equal intervals of time
Graph is not a straight line

concepts.lesson: Speed is the distance travelled by an object per unit time. It tells how fast an object is moving.
concepts.real_example: If a car travels 100 km in 2 hours, its speed is 50 km/h.

concepts.lesson: Velocity is the speed of an object in a specific direction. It describes both how fast and in which direction an object moves.
concepts.real_example: A car moving at 60 km/h towards the north has velocity.
key_points: Changes if direction or speed changes

concepts.lesson: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with time. It occurs when an object increases or decreases its speed or changes direction.
concepts.real_example: A bicycle speeding up when a rider pedals faster shows acceleration.
key_points: Change in velocity per unit time
Occurs in speeding up or slowing down

concepts.lesson: A distance-time graph shows how distance changes with time. It helps in understanding the type of motion of an object.
concepts.real_example: A straight line on a distance-time graph represents uniform motion.

concepts.lesson: A velocity-time graph represents how velocity changes with time. The area under the graph gives the displacement of the object.
concepts.real_example: If velocity remains constant, the graph is a straight horizontal line.

concepts.lesson: The equations of motion describe the relationship between velocity, acceleration, time, and distance for objects moving with uniform acceleration.
concepts.real_example: These equations help calculate the distance travelled by a moving car.

concepts.lesson: Uniform circular motion occurs when an object moves along a circular path with constant speed. Even though speed is constant, velocity changes because direction changes.
concepts.real_example: The motion of the Earth around the Sun is an example of uniform circular motion.
